preview_text:
The model rates Croatia as a heavy favorite with a 79% win probability, compared to Ghana's 3% chance. The gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast—Croatia is projected to dominate this matchup decisively under the statistical framework employed.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Croatia favored but not overwhelmingly so in draw likelihood. A draw accounts for 18% of outcomes in the model, suggesting the match could remain competitive despite the large win gap. Ghana's low win probability indicates the model sees little path to victory, though the 18-point cushion for draws reflects residual uncertainty in a single-match fixture.

A meaningful gap separates the model from both sportsbooks. The model assigns Croatia 79% while the market consensus sits at 57–59%, a 20-point divergence favoring the underdog's chances in market prices. The draw sits slightly higher in market assessments (25% vs. 18%), and Ghana receives substantially more support (15–17%) than the model projects (3%). The dashboard makes no claim about which source is more accurate; users should weigh both estimates when forming their own view.
